decisions.briefing: Markets drifted flat today — NASDAQ unchanged, S&P 500 up a tenth of a percent — a quiet consolidation session with no clear catalyst. Cash sits at $477, just below the $500 buy threshold, so no new positions are opened today. Existing holdings are left to run; the stop-loss and profit-target system handles any automatic exits.

market_education.summary_en: U.S. equities logged a near-flat session on June 26, 2026, with the S&P 500 edging up 0.1% and the NASDAQ finishing unchanged, suggesting neither bulls nor bears had a strong enough catalyst to press their case. Volume was light heading into the end of the month, a common pattern as institutional investors rebalance quarter-end portfolios rather than initiate new directional bets. The lack of major economic data releases or central bank commentary left markets in a holding pattern, with momentum names maintaining modest technical strength without breaking out.
market_education.summary_zh: 2026年6月26日，美國股市幾乎持平，標普500指數小漲0.1%，納斯達克收盤幾乎不變，顯示多空雙方均缺乏足夠的催化劑推動行情。由於臨近季末，機構投資者多以再平衡投資組合為主，而非主動建立新倉位，市場成交量因此偏淡。主要經濟數據及聯儲局官員發言均付之闕如，市場進入觀望格局，動能型個股技術面雖維持穩健，但未見明顯突破。

daily_lesson.explanation_en: Cash drag refers to the performance cost of holding uninvested cash in a portfolio. While cash provides safety and optionality, it earns near-zero returns compared to equities, which slowly erodes overall portfolio performance during rising markets. Today's situation — $477 sitting just below the $500 buy threshold — is a small but real example: those idle dollars aren't compounding alongside the rest of the portfolio.
daily_lesson.explanation_zh: 「現金拖累」（Cash Drag）是指投資組合中閒置現金對整體表現所造成的拖累效應。現金雖然提供安全性與靈活性，但在股市上漲期間，其近乎零的回報率會逐漸侵蝕組合的整體收益。今日情況即為典型例子——477美元現金略低於500美元的買入門檻，這筆閒置資金無法跟隨其餘持倉一同複利增長，形成小而真實的現金拖累。
